title: primula viscosa subsp. graveolens
intro: Primula viscosa subsp. graveolens, also known as sticky primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Primula viscosa subsp. graveolens include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help to boost the immune system and protect against infections. Primula viscosa subsp. graveolens can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in folk remedies for various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
